entry.title: Microsoft Teams as a Delivery Channel for My Metrics Reports in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP Analytics Cloud has continuously evolved its alerting and monitoring capabilities for the My Metrics List feature. Users can already receive scheduled report notifications through two established channels: the SAC Notification Center and email inbox. These channels ensure that business users stay informed about metric movements on a schedule that fits their workflow.

With the 2026 Q2 release, SAP Analytics Cloud extends this capability further by introducing Microsoft Teams as a supported delivery channel. Business users can now receive their My Metrics report notifications directly within Teams — the collaboration hub where they already spend a significant portion of their working day. This eliminates the need to switch between applications to access critical metric updates and act on them quickly.

Feature Capabilities

The Microsoft Teams delivery channel is designed to be fully consistent with the existing notification scope available today via email reports and the SAC Notification Center. The following capabilities are included:

  • Channel Configuration:  Users can select Microsoft Teams as a notification option when configuring their Metrics Report subscription. The option is available within the Distribution List step of the subscription wizard.
  • Teams Channel Email Integration:  The integration leverages Teams channel email addresses (e.g. <channelname>@<tenant>.teams.ms). Users enter the channel email into the 'Send Emails to Microsoft Teams Channel' dialog — no additional connector or app installation is required.  For sending an email to channel in Microsoft Teams please refer to here
  • Notification Content:  Each Teams notification includes all standard report details: Metrics List Name, Subscription Name, Recurrence Pattern, Start Date, End Date, an Overview of Metrics table with Latest Value, Changes in Value, Previous Value, and Latest Variance — plus clickable metric name links and a direct 'Open My Metrics' deep-link to SAC.
  • Scheduling and Trigger Respect:  Teams notifications honour existing scheduling configurations and trigger conditions set during subscription setup. No separate scheduling is needed for the Teams channel.
  • Reply in Thread:  Teams thread functionality is preserved — users can reply inline to a notification post, enabling quick discussion or follow-up directly in context.

Getting Started

Configuration is completed directly within the existing 'Subscribe to Metrics Report' wizard in SAP Analytics Cloud. No additional plugins, connectors, or admin setup are required beyond having the Teams channel email address available.

  1. Open the Subscription Wizard  From your My Metrics list, click the options menu (⋯) on any metric card and select 'Subscribe'. The 'Subscribe to Metrics Report' dialog opens. Open Subscription Dialog

     

     

  2. Configure the Schedule  In Step 2, set the Recurrence Pattern (e.g. Daily), the Start Date of First Occurrence, and the End Date. Use 'Check Availability' to validate the schedule.Define Occurence

     

     

  3. Add the Teams Channel as a Recipient  In Step 3 (Distribution List), click '+ Add Recipients', then select 'Send Emails to Microsoft Teams Channel'. Enter the Teams channel email address (available from Teams under Get email address for the channel) and click Save.Add email address of Team channel

     

     

  4. Save the Subscription  Once the Teams channel email is added to the Recipients list (alongside any individual user recipients), click Save. The subscription is now active and will deliver to both the Teams channel and any individual recipients.Save the subscription report

     

     

  5. Metrics Report in Microsoft TeamsMetrics Report in Teams Channel

     

     

     

Summary and Outlook

With the 2026 Q2 release, SAP Analytics Cloud adds Microsoft Teams as a delivery channel for My Metrics reports. Users can now receive scheduled notifications — including subscription details, a full metrics overview, and a direct link to SAC — directly in Teams, alongside the existing SAC Notification Center and email options. Setup requires no additional integration; users simply add a Teams channel email address in the existing subscription wizard.

 A lot more is coming for My Metrics and Metrics Report, and I'm excited about what's ahead. Stay tuned for updates in the near future. Until then, I'd love to hear your thoughts — leave a comment, share your thoughts. That conversation is what keeps us pointed in the right direction.

entry.title: Variable Pay-Requirement-Two different bonus target percentages
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

                 I am working on a new requirement from a customer where they use 2 different bonus target percentages one is called company based target percentage , which is very much like target bonus percentage but other one is Individual incentive percentage. Both the values are specific to an employee and can vary during the year based on various conditions like job level, location, manager etc it's maintained by the admins. 

Overall requirement shared by business 

Total Annual Variable Pay Amount = Employee's Annual Base Salary × [Component Tracks] × Time Factor × Individual Performance Multiplier
  • Track 1: Target % of Company based target × Business Score
  • Track 2: Target % of Annual Individual Performance Incentive
Example Table
Annual Base Salary Company based target: Target % Company based target: Business Score Annual Individual Performance Incentive: Target % Individual Performance Multiplier
Annual Base Salary 100,000Company based Target 8.0%Business Score Global: 110%Individual performance incentive 12.5%Individual performance evaluation 90%
     
Assuming employee was eligible for full year 100%
track1- Company Based Target
  • Calculation: 100,000 X 8% X 110% X 100% X 90%
  • Total: 7,920
Track2- Annual Individual Performance Incentive
  • Calculation: 100,000 X 12.5% X 100% X 90%
  • Total: 11,250
Grand Total Payout
  • 7,920 + 11,250 = 19,170

 In variable pay setup , I can understand we can try to set it up using 2 different business plans but we do not have option to setup 2 separate basis calculation formulas. Has someone encountered such a need, especially for customers from China? would be great to know the thoughts from the experts.

 @xavierlegarrec - Your thoughts on this would be appreciated.

SAP SuccessFactors Compensation

entry.title: 4-Eyes by Configuration: Multi-Processor Workflow Approvals in SAP S/4HANA 2608
entry.content:
Introduction

A quick note before diving in: this blog was initially drafted with the help of SAP Joule for Consultants. — a great example of how AI tools can meaningfully accelerate the writing process.  And improved with Claude Sonnet. That said, all content, accuracy, and opinions here are entirely my own responsibility.

A special thank you to the colleagues who built and shipped these features under significant time pressure. Great software doesn’t happen without great teamwork — and it ultimately takes dedicated engineers to turn ideas into delivered, production-ready capabilities.

In today’s compliance-driven enterprise landscape, ensuring that critical business decisions are never made unilaterally is both a governance imperative and, in many industries, a regulatory requirement. Workflow automation has long been a cornerstone of SAP’s approach to streamlining approvals — but automation without embedded controls can introduce new risks rather than eliminate them.

Two foundational control mechanisms address this challenge directly:

  • Multi-Processor Task Completion — ensuring that a defined number of independent individuals must each process a task before a workflow can continue
  • Approver Exclusion (4-Eyes Principle) — ensuring that the person who created a business document cannot also approve it (for details on this capability, see the linked resource)

With SAP S/4HANA 2608 (and 2702 for customers on that release cycle), SAP has introduced both of these capabilities natively within the Manage Workflow application and the broader flexible workflow framework — empowering Business Process Experts to configure robust dual-control and multi-control approval processes without writing a single line of code.

But governance is only as strong as its weakest configuration. That is why this release also introduces a new Validation Callback method that prevents misconfigured workflow definitions from ever being activated — closing the gap between documentation-based guidance and system-enforced compliance.

This blog covers all three features comprehensively: what they are, why they were introduced, how they work, and how to configure them correctly.

Background & Motivation

The Governance Gap in Workflow Automation

Prior to these enhancements, the Manage Workflows application offered two task completion modes for workflow step recipients:

Mode Runtime Behavior Limitation

One recipientA single shared task instance is created; the first person to act completes the stepNo guarantee of independent review
All recipientsA separate task instance is created for each recipient; every recipient must complete their taskInflexible — requires full group participation with no middle ground

Neither mode offered the precision required for a true four-eyes or six-eyes principle. Organizations needing exactly two or three independent completions had to rely on the "run with previous" feature combined with manual process discipline or documentation-based guidance — all of which are fragile controls that do not scale in enterprise environments.

Multi-Processor Task Completion (4-Eyes / 6-Eyes via Configuration)

What Is It?

The Multi-Processor Task Completion feature addresses this gap directly: it ensures that a defined number of independent individuals each complete their own task instance before the workflow continues.

This is the mechanism that enables a true four-eyes principle (2 processors) or six-eyes principle (3 processors) in scenarios where multiple parallel approvers must each independently act — not just one of them, and not necessarily all of them.

Task Instance Behavior: The Three Modes

Completion mode is configured in the Recipients section of a workflow step using a radio button group:

Mode Runtime Behavior

One recipientA single shared task instance is created; the first recipient to act completes the step
Individual task per recipient — AllA multi-instance task is created; all recipients must complete the task
Two or three recipientsA multi-instance task is created; the defined number of recipients must approve, or one must reject

The two or three recipients option is the key addition in releases 2608/2702. It fills the gap between "one person acts" and "everyone must act" — enabling precise control over how many independent completions are required before the workflow proceeds.

Recipient Assignment Types

The recipient configuration UI is used consistently across steps, notifications, and other workflow artifacts. Supported assignment types include:

  • Agent Rule — dynamically resolves recipients based on business rules
  • Team — assigns the task to a predefined team (configured via Manage Teams and Responsibilities)
  • Concrete User — directly specifies one or more named users

Note: If you select the two or three recipients mode but assign only one concrete user, the system will display a validation error.

 Runtime Design

Several new UI elements have been introduced to support transparency at runtime:

  • A status bar at the task instance level shows who is currently processing a task within a team — and clearly indicates who has already approved.

 

  • The workflow instance component now includes an improved status bar displaying the number of required processors versus the number who have already acted.

     These additions give both approvers and process administrators real-time visibility into the state of multi-processor steps.

The Uniqueness Rule: One Person, One Completion

A critical safeguard of this feature is that the same person cannot complete more than one parallel task instance. This is what makes the four-eyes and six-eyes principles meaningful — it is not sufficient for two task instances to exist; they must be completed by two different people.

This uniqueness rule applies even in edge cases involving substitution and forwarding:

Scenario Behavior

SubstitutionIf Person A completes a task as a substitute for Person B, Person A cannot also complete their own parallel task instance
ForwardingIf Person A forwards their task to Person C, and Person C already has their own parallel instance, the uniqueness rule still applies
DelegationThe initiator cannot act as a delegatee for any parallel task instance in the same step

Resolution for blocked users: If a person is unable to complete their own task because the uniqueness rule applies (e.g., they already acted via substitution on a parallel instance), they can — where permitted — forward their own task instance to another eligible person. This self-service resolution requires no administrative intervention.

An explicit error message is displayed when a user attempts to complete a second task instance in violation of this rule.

Validation Callback for Mandatory Step Configurations

The Problem It Solves

The introduction of the N-processor option increases the risk that a workflow modeler will inadvertently misconfigure a step that must always be set to "All of the recipients." The "Verify Intercompany General Journal Entry" step is the primary example — but the pattern applies to any scenario where a specific completion mode is a non-negotiable compliance requirement.

Previously, this requirement was enforced only through documentation. With the 2608 release, a validation callback mechanism now prevents the activation of a workflow definition if a mandatory step is incorrectly configured — catching errors before they affect live business processes.

How the Validation Works

The validation is implemented via the if_swf_flex_ifs_def_appl~on_validation_callback method. When a workflow modeler attempts to activate a workflow definition, the system executes this callback. If the VerifyMultiInstance step is not configured to require all processors (cv_all_processors), the system raises a validation exception and blocks activation.

This represents a significant improvement over documentation-only guidance:

  • It operates at activation time, not at runtime — catching errors before they affect live processes
  • It is automatic and non-bypassable — a modeler cannot accidentally skip the validation
  • It provides a clear error message directing the modeler to correct the configuration
  • It scales — the same pattern can be applied to any scenario where specific configurations are mandatory

Code Reference (Baseline for Implementation)

The following ABAP snippet serves as a baseline for implementing the validation callback:

METHOD if_swf_flex_ifs_def_appl~on_validation_callback.  
DATA(lt_activity) = io_workflow->get_all_activities( ).  
" Raise a validation error if the multi-instance step is not configured to require all processors.  
LOOP AT lt_activity INTO DATA(lo_activity).    
IF lo_activity->get_step_id( ) = 'VerifyMultiInstance'.      
DATA(lv_number_of_proc) = lo_activity->get_number_of_requ_processors( ).      
IF lv_number_of_proc <> lo_activity->cv_all_processors.        
RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE cx_swf_flex_ifs_exception          
MESSAGE ID 'Your Message Class'
TYPE 'E'          
NUMBER 'Your Message Number'.      
ENDIF.    
ENDIF.  
ENDLOOP.
ENDMETHOD.

Scope and Intentional Boundaries

It is important to understand the deliberate scope boundaries of this feature:

In Scope Out of Scope

Validation at activation time that prevents an invalid workflow definition from being activatedA UX-level change that hides or disables invalid configuration options in the UI (e.g., greying out the "2 processors" option for the VerifyMultiInstance step)

Discussions are already underway to explore UX improvements that would disable invalid options directly in the configuration interface.

Combined Use & Real-World Scenarios

When to Use Which Feature

Understanding when to apply each feature — and how they complement each other — is key to designing effective compliance-oriented workflows:

Requirement Recommended Feature

Prevent the initiator from approving their own requestExcluded Agents
Require exactly 2 independent people to each complete a taskTwo recipients mode
Require exactly 3 independent people to each complete a taskThree recipients mode
Require all members of a group to complete a taskAll recipients mode
Prevent a specific step from being misconfigured at activationValidation Callback
Combine initiator exclusion with multi-processor completion4-Eyes Principle + Multi-Processor (both active simultaneously)

In many real-world scenarios, all three features will be active simultaneously — delivering layered, system-enforced governance across the full approval lifecycle.

Example: Financial Processes — Journal Entry Postings

For multi-level verification scenarios — such as a 4-level approval hierarchy based on distinct monetary thresholds across multiple company codes — workflows can be defined with sequentially ordered steps, each with its own amount-based precondition and assigned approver group. The multi-processor task completion feature ensures that each level of that hierarchy meets the required independent review count.

Configuration Checklist & Getting Started

Prerequisites and System Requirements

  • SAP S/4HANA 2608 (on-premise) or 2702 (for customers on that release cycle)
  • Flexible Workflow must be active and configured for the relevant business object
  • Users must be set up as business users with appropriate authorizations
  • The Manage Teams and Responsibilities application is recommended for agent determination
  • For scenario developers: the Validation Callback is implemented via the on_validation_callback method

Best Practices

  • Always test your workflow configuration using the Simulation feature (available from SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408 onwards) — verify which workflow definition triggers for a given business object instance before going live
  • Explicitly configure excluded agents to avoid workflow stalls or compliance gaps when the initiator is the only eligible approver
  • Define clear preconditions for each step in multi-step scenarios to avoid unintended overlaps in approval routing

entry.content:

(1) Visit the SAP Universal ID website 

(2) sign up for an account using your company email, and

Most times, there are variations in business section weight vs. Individual section weight but it is based on data such as job level or pay grade. My first thought is to push back a little and see if there is any way to standardize some of the percentages to make the configuration based on something other than employee id. If that does not work, because the calculation seems to be formulaic and does not have discretion, I might try to configure as a base X business plan with many custom fields using ALCF to complete the calculations

entry.title: Re: While doing MIRO business got the error negative vaule
entry.content:
If your MIRO document is parked (so not 'as already posted') but posting fails due to this error, check:

  • Stock quantity and stock movements to confirm actual available stock.
  • Whether relevant SAP Notes/Support Packages for your system version have been applied that address this issue.
  • That pricing and quantities on the invoice match the stock situation.

Check also:

  • 3123287 - MIRO: Moving average price for material would be negative ( Part 1) 
  • 3667067 - Negative Moving Average Price - Enhancements for Error Message M8 783

entry.title: Re: Validation to block cancellation of AR invoice for sp...
entry.content:
This version is for HANA and only allows the “manager” user to cancel

IF :object_type in ('13', '17', '23') AND :transaction_type = 'A' THEN

    DECLARE temp_var_0 INT := 0;

    SELECT COUNT(B."DocEntry") INTO temp_var_0
    FROM "OINV" B
    LEFT OUTER JOIN "OUSR" C ON B."UserSign" = C."USERID"
    WHERE B."CANCELED" IN ('C','Y')
      AND C."U_NAME" NOT IN ('manager')
      AND B."DocEntry" = :list_of_cols_val_tab_del;

    IF temp_var_0 > 0 THEN
        error := -1099;
        error_message := 'No tiene autorizacion para Cancelar documentos, llame a Gerencia';
    END IF;

END IF;

entry.title: Re: Unable to Remove House Bank Assignment – Error "House...
entry.content:
#GenAI-Assisted Content

Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. 

I asked our internal chatbot for support, and here's the response. Please check whether the suggestions help you resolve your issue:

Solution for Reassigning Bank Accounts to New House Banks

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, if a house bank account is already in use, it cannot be directly deleted or reassigned due to existing dependencies such as accounting documents, payment programs, or bank statements. However, you can follow these steps to achieve your business requirement:

  1. Close the Current Bank Accounts:

    • You need to close the existing bank accounts that are linked to House Bank 9983. This involves sending a closing request and confirming the closure in the system.

  2. Create New Bank Accounts:

    • After closing the existing accounts, you can create new bank accounts and assign them to the newly created house banks.

  3. Copy Bank Account Data:

    • You can copy the data from the closed bank accounts to the new ones to ensure continuity in your financial operations.

  4. Update Configuration Settings:

    • Ensure that all relevant configuration settings are updated to reflect the new house bank assignments. This includes updating payment programs and any other settings that reference the old house bank accounts.

  5. Use SSCUI for Message Control:

    • If you encounter error messages during this process, you can change these messages to warnings using the SSCUI ‘Change Message Control for Bank Account Management’ (ID 103894). This allows you to bypass certain validations temporarily.

For detailed steps on closing and reopening bank accounts, refer to the SAP documentation on Closing Bank Accounts. Cash and Liquidity Management - Closing Bank Accounts

Important Considerations

  • Ensure that all transactions related to the old house bank accounts are completed before initiating the closure.
  • Be aware that linking multiple house bank accounts to a single bank account can lead to unintended side effects in certain apps.

By following these steps, you can effectively reassign your bank accounts to new house banks while maintaining compliance with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud’s operational constraints.

For more details, see the following information:

[1] Error messages indicating the deletion/change of house bank account assigned in a bank account is not possible
[2] Set Up bank determination for Payment Transactions not possible to delete error
[3] Cash and Liquidity Management - Closing Bank Accounts

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Clarification on MRP Live (MD01N) Behavior for P V Se...
entry.content:
Here are answers to your questions on MRP Live (MD01N) Production Version determination and BOM explosion:

  1. Prerequisite conditions for Production Version determination and BOM explosion
  • A valid Production Version must exist in C223 with a BOM and routing assigned.
  • The validity period of the Production Version (MKAL‑ADATU/BDATU) must cover the MRP requirement date.
  • The BOM itself must be valid on the same requirement date.
  • Lot size and plant assignments must be consistent.
  • Without a valid Production Version, MRP Live cannot perform BOM explosion.
  1. Which date is used for validity checks?
  • The system uses the MRP requirement date (PBED‑PBDAT) to validate both the Production Version and BOM.
  • It does not use:
    • Purchase Requisition Delivery Date (EBAN‑LFDAT)
    • Purchase Requisition Release Date (EBAN‑FRGDT)
    • Purchase Requisition Creation Date (EBAN‑BADAT)
  • The requirement date generated during MRP planning is the decisive factor.
  1. Standard Documentation
  • SAP Note 2267880 – S4TWL – BOM, Routing, Production Version: Explains that Production Versions are mandatory in S/4HANA MRP Live and BOM explosion is only possible through them.
  • Transaction MD_MRP_FORCE_CLASSIC: Provides logs and diagnostic information when MRP Live cannot determine a valid Production Version or BOM.

So the Summary is MRP Live checks Production Version and BOM validity against the MRP requirement date (PBED‑PBDAT). If validity fails, you’ll see messages like “No valid production version found” or “No valid BOM”. To avoid incomplete PRs, ensure Production Versions and BOMs are valid for the planning horizon and correctly maintained in C223.

entry.title: Re: Invoice summery can not be sent to customer via e-mail
entry.content:
#GenAI-Assisted Content

Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. 

I asked our internal chatbot for support, and here's the response. Please check whether the suggestions help you resolve your issue:

Output Channel Error

When you encounter an error stating that the system does not support output channel ‘00010’, it typically means that the selected channel is not configured or supported for the specific output type you are trying to use. In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, the supported output channels are: 

  • EMAIL: Allows flexible configuration of senders and recipients as well as the use of email templates.
  • PRINT: Uses the SAP spool system to print documents.
  • EXTOM: Sends raw data from the standard output process to an external system.
  • EDI: Sends electronic messages to business receivers.

If ‘00010’ is not one of these supported channels, you will need to select a valid channel for your output type. You can configure the output channels in the ‘Output Parameter Determination’ app to ensure the correct channel is selected for your output type.

For more details, see the following information:

Manage Your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Output Channels

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: DRC for India GSTR reports
entry.content:
Hi all,

Please note that basic statutory reporting features are included with SAP S/4HANA Cloud. However, advanced statutory reporting and compliance capabilities require an additional SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) license.

The license material SAP Document and Reporting Compliance S4HC (8013658) is associated with scope item 5XU, which provides additional DRC functionality. For more information about scope item 5XU, please refer to the following link:

https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-013/2602/SolP/5XU?region=IN

For confirmation of the licenses purchased in your landscape and for any licensing-related questions, please contact your SAP Account Manager.

 

Additionally, configuration activity '101071 – Setting Up Your Statutory Reporting' is required for statutory reporting setup (for example, for an India reporting entity). This configuration activity is dependent on scope item 1J2.

 

Kindly refer the below help document for more information about the India GSTR reports,

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/634261119fec4d58970471f2c4a9a740/b9524d0554714ab6b0dacc8866c0fac9.html?version=LATEST&locale=en-US

 

Best regards,

Atanu

SAP Support - S/4HANA Cloud

entry.title: Re: What’s New in SAP HANA Cloud – March 2026
entry.content:
@thomashammer 

This blog is the March 2026 release overview for SAP HANA Cloud. It covers key updates across AI agents, modeling, administration, and performance.

Highlights:

  • Discovery Agent + Data Agent (beta): natural-language data discovery and execution using a database object knowledge graph.

  • Semantic onboarding of calculation views into SAP Business Data Cloud: reuse existing HANA Cloud models without data movement.

  • Performance Analysis app in SAP HANA Cloud Central: guided workflows for CPU, memory, lock issues, plus an on-demand chart library.

  • Text classification improvements in the predictive analysis library: better performance, efficiency, and automated ML for text features.

  • Elastic Compute Node policy: new ECN policy for better management and control of compute resources.

  • Additional ML enhancements (feature agglomeration, affinity propagation clustering) and data center expansions.

entry.title: Re: [S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition] Multi-approver workflo...
entry.content:
#GenAI-Assisted Content

Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. 

I asked our internal chatbot for support, and here's the response. Please check whether the suggestions help you resolve your issue:

Multi-Approver Workflow for Supplier Invoices by Cost Center in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

To implement a multi-approver workflow for supplier invoices based on cost centers in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you can follow these steps:

1. Standard Mechanism for Multi-Approver Setup

Currently, the standard mechanism in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition does not directly support assigning multiple approvers based on cost centers for supplier invoices. The ‘Responsible Person’ field in cost center master data only allows a single person to be assigned.

2. Using ‘Manage Teams and Responsibilities’ App

The ‘Manage Teams and Responsibilities’ app can be used to define teams and assign functions to team members,  but it does not directly map to supplier invoice approval against a cost center. This app is typically used for defining roles and responsibilities in other contexts, such as journal entry verification.

3. Workflow Configuration for Supplier Invoices

You can configure flexible workflows for supplier invoices using the ‘Manage Workflows for Supplier Invoices’ app.  This app allows you to define condition-based workflows for approval processes, including multi-step approvals. However, the configuration for cost center-based approval is not directly supported.

4. Guidance and Documentation

For more detailed guidance, you can refer to the SAP Help Portal and community blogs that discuss flexible workflows and their configurations. These resources can provide insights into how to set up workflows for supplier invoices, although they may not cover cost center-specific scenarios directly.

If you need a custom solution, you might consider using extensibility options or consulting with SAP experts to explore potential customizations or workarounds.

Conclusion

While the standard S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition does not directly support multi-approver workflows for supplier invoices based on cost centers, you can explore flexible workflows and team configurations for other types of approvals. For specific requirements, custom solutions may be necessary.

For more details, see the following information:

A Configuration Example of How to Optimize Journal Entry Verification

Flexible Workflow for Supplier Invoices

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: credit note and Debit note
entry.content:
#GenAI-Assisted Content

Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. 

I asked our internal chatbot for support, and here's the response. Please check whether the suggestions help you resolve your issue:

Round‑off Calculation for Credit Note and Debit Note Documents

  • General behaviour

    • Rounding is a configuration option that applies to the total amount (including VAT/GST) of an invoicing document, whether it is an invoice, a credit memo (credit note) or a debit memo (debit note).
    • When the rounding function is active, the rounded total appears in its own line on the document, and the rounding difference is automatically distributed to the individual line items.
    • The same rounding key and rules are used for both debit‑type and credit‑type amounts; the configuration defines separate “Debit Rounding Unit” and “Credit Rounding Unit” values, but in the examples provided they are set to the same value.

  • Rounding rules by currency (as defined in the “Rounding Rules” view)

    Currency Debit Rounding Unit Credit Rounding Unit Amount Method (both) Example of rounding
    Swedish Krona (SEK)1.001.00“Round up or down to the nearest whole number.”138,22 SEK → 138,00 SEK; 138,68 SEK → 139,00 SEK; 138,50 SEK → 139,00 SEK
    Singapore Dollar (SGD)1.001.00“Round to the nearest 5 cent.”138.22 SGD → 138.20 SGD; 138.68 SGD → 138.70 SGD
    Swiss Franc (CHF)0.050.05“Round to the nearest whole number” (applied to 0.05‑CHF increments).582.02 CHF → 582.00 CHF; 582.03 CHF → 582.05 CHF

  • Key configuration steps (common to all currencies)

    1. Open Define Key for Invoice Rounding (ID 104810).
    2. In the Rounding Key view, confirm the C (Convergent FI‑CA) application area and enter the rounding key description.
    3. On the Rounding Key tab, specify transaction data for rounding carry‑forward and write‑off.
    4. In the Rounding Rules view, enter the relevant currency and set the debit/credit rounding units and methods as shown above.
    5. Save the entries.
    6. Assign the rounding key to the appropriate invoicing process via Assign Key for Invoice Rounding (ID 104811).

  • What the context does not specify

    • The documents do not provide a separate, detailed example for a debit note (debit memo) rounding calculation; they only illustrate rounding for invoices and credit memos.
    • Therefore, the exact rounding outcome for a debit note would follow the same unit and method defined for the “Debit Rounding Unit” in the relevant currency configuration.

The above information is derived directly from the provided configuration documentation for rounding in Convergent Invoicing.

In case this information doesn't help you resolve your issue, please let us know.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Adding custom field in "Receive Products" app
entry.content:
Dear @KhaingZinTun,

You're not limited to static fields, what matters is where the logic runs:

  • Frontend logic / custom actions (controller extensions): not available the app's synchronous views disable them in the adaptation project
  • Backend logic to populate field data: supported, via the app's released BAdIs

For your case, pulling Product Master Data into new fields should be handled through the Product Details Enhancement BAdI RETAIL_ST_PL_PRODUCT_ENH. This BAdI enriches the product data in the backend, and a UI field can bind to that data only if the data is exposed in the app’s data model/OData response

More details below:

  • SAP Help – Extensibility: Look Up Retail Products
  • SAP Help – Controller Extensions

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Reset of CBC configuration in customizing tenant necessary (for CBC Workspace "DEV - Customizing")
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

we are currently in the early Explore phase of our SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud implementation project and have just started configuration activities in Central Business Configuration (CBC) for our Development System respectively Customizing tenant.

During the initial scoping and deployment, we unfortunately missed including the scope items:
- 5HG
- 5KF

Based on the official “Availability Dependencies” documentation, these scope items cannot be added afterwards to an already deployed CBC workspace.

Since we are still at a very early stage (no productive configuration or dependent activities completed yet), we kindly request your support with one of the following options:

1. A full CBC Reset of our workspace  

   Affected systems / URLs:
   - Frontend (S/4HANA):  
        - CBC Backend:  
     OR

2. If a reset is not recommended or technically feasible, please advise on the best alternative approach, such as:
   - Re-provisioning of the CBC workspace / Customizing tenant (component:  XX‑S4C‑OPR‑SRV)
   - Creating a new workspace with correct initial scoping
   - Any SAP-recommended procedure to include the missing scope items

Our main objective is to ensure a clean and correct project setup before proceeding further in the Explore phase.

As we are still at a very early stage, data loss or reinitialization is fully acceptable.

We appreciate your guidance on the most efficient and recommended way forward.

Best regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Reset of CBC configuration in customizing tenant nece...
entry.content:
Hi!

Thank you for your question.

As we understand, you identified that scope items 5HG and 5KF were not selected during the initial scoping activities in your SAP Central Business Configuration workspace.

The good news is that, if your workspace is still in the early implementation stages and the deployment has not yet been executed via Deploy Scope and Organizational Structure activity in CBC, there is no need for a tenant reset. In this situation, the recommended approach is to restart the setup process by deleting the workspace and perform the initial scoping again, ensuring that all required scope items are included from the beginning.

This is generally the most efficient path forward, as it allows you to establish the correct project scope before any downstream configuration activities, deployments, or dependencies are introduced and helps avoid potential complications later in the implementation.

As described in KBA 3377871 - Not possible to activate scope items 5HG and 5KF in SAP Central Business Configuration - SAP for Me, when deployment has not yet occurred, recreating the setup with the correct scope selection is the preferred approach. Once the required scope items have been included, you can proceed with the project activities as planned.

This is mentioned in "If the scope items 5HG and 5KF were not activated during the initial scoping phase and no content was deployed to the target system via activity Deploy Scope and Organizational Structure, the Workspace can be deleted and recreated from scratch. For more details, refer to SAP Help Portal SAP Help - Deleting a Workspace."

We hope this clarifies the available options and helps you move forward with confidence.

Best Regards!

entry.title: Re: ECP Custom Development
entry.content: In addition to what others have said (i.e., basically yes, you can have some custom developments) - for details on what is permitted, I would like to refer you to the scope, implementation, and service catalog documents in the ECP product page (SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll | SAP Help Portal | SAP Help Portal).  Basically, customizations should not interfere with Support Package implementation and should be related to the payroll use-case.  What you really need to avoid is direct modifications on SAP standard objects.

entry.title: Re: Project Cost Line Items – Loss of Cumulative and Commitment-Based Measures in Recent Releases
entry.content:
When raising this concern through official product support channels, the standard response (KBA 3742776 - APPS F6991 and F6992 do not display Commitments) states that the new applications are working as designed.

The recommended workaround provided by Product Support is to steer users back to the classical SAP GUI Report Writer/Painter transactions:

  • S_ALR_8701354 (Project Info System: Costs/Revenues/Expenditures/Funds)
  • S_ALR_87013542 (Actual/Commitment/Total/Plan in Project)

As SAP specialists striving to implement SAP Activate best practices and driving Clean Core governance, this workaround presents an architectural contradiction. We are pushing organizations to adopt the modern Fiori UX and deprecate legacy GUI reporting, yet the standard digital core forces a regression to 20-year-old ABAP report engines to perform core financial cross-module validations (PS-FI-CO-MM).
